description: Document what Jidoka owns, including lifecycle coordination, prompt assembly, memory, compaction, MCP, web safety, subagents, handoffs, workflows, schedules, imports, tracing, debugging, AgentView, and Kino. Document what remains Jido/Jido.AI owned, including model calls, ReAct execution, provider behavior, signals, threads, and durable runtime storage.
title: Document Jidoka versus Jido ownership boundaries
